Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product in the inventory system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: Stock keeping unit; a unique identifier for inventory tracking.

quantity.description: Current quantity of the product in stock.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the product is currently active and available for sale.

InventoryMovement.title: InventoryMovement
InventoryMovement.description: Represents a movement of inventory, such as a sale, purchase, or adjustment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InventoryMovement entity.

quantityChange.description: The amount by which the inventory changed (positive for increase, negative for decrease).

movementDate.description: The date and time the inventory movement occurred.

movementType.description: The type of inventory movement (e.g., sale, purchase, adjustment).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Sale entity.

customerId.description: Reference to Customer. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Sale).  Default to 'Eventual Customer' if no customer is specified.

saleDate.description: The date and time of the sale.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the sale.

paymentMethod.description: The method of payment used for the sale.

SaleLineItem.title: SaleLineItem
SaleLineItem.description: Represents a single line item within a sale transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SaleLineItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product sold in this line item.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time of sale.

totalPrice.description: The total price of the line item (quantity * unitPrice).

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Represents a purchase order for restocking inventory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Purchase entity.

purchaseDate.description: The date the purchase order was created.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the purchase order.

PurchaseLineItem.title: PurchaseLineItem
PurchaseLineItem.description: Represents a single line item within a purchase order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PurchaseLineItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product purchased in this line item.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time of purchase.

totalPrice.description: The total price of the line item (quantity * unitPrice).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the customer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

contactName.description: The name of the contact person at the supplier.

contactEmail.description: The email address of the contact person at the supplier.

contactPhone.description: The phone number of the contact person at the supplier.

TaxType.title: TaxType
TaxType.description: Represents a type of tax that can be applied to products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TaxType entity.

name.description: The name of the tax type (e.g., VAT, Sales Tax).

Configuration.title: Configuration
Configuration.description: Represents configuration settings for the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Configuration entity.

commerceName.description: The name of the commerce or business.

address.description: The address of the commerce or business.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the commerce or business.

securityPin.description: Security PIN to protect the system (should be hashed before storing).

printerType.description: The type of printer used for receipts (e.g., 57mm).

definition.description: Stores product information. Includes 'id' as productId.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Stores inventory movement records. Includes 'id' as inventoryMovementId.

params.name: inventoryMovementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inventory movement.

definition.description: Stores sales transaction records. Includes 'id' as saleId.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sale.

definition.description: Stores individual line items for sales transactions. Includes 'id' as saleLineItemId.

params.name: saleLineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sale line item.

definition.description: Stores purchase order records. Includes 'id' as purchaseId.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchase.

definition.description: Stores individual line items for purchase orders. Includes 'id' as purchaseLineItemId.

params.name: purchaseLineItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the purchase line item.

definition.description: Stores customer information. Includes 'id' as customerId.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Stores supplier information. Includes 'id' as supplierId.

params.name: supplierId
params.description: The unique identifier for the supplier.

definition.description: Stores tax type information. Includes 'id' as taxTypeId.

params.name: taxTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tax type.

definition.description: Stores application configuration settings. Includes 'id' as configurationId.  Only a single document would exist here, or a document per user.

params.name: configurationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the configuration.

definition.description: Stores the history of currency exchange rates.

params.name: rateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the currency rate entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the inventory and point-of-sale web application "TIENDA FACIL WEB" using a flat structure that emphasizes authorization independence and straightforward security rules.  The design focuses on clarity and scalability, making it easy to secure individual documents and collections.

Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. While not explicitly needed based on the current entities, if future requirements dictate subcollections with access dependent on a parent document's attributes, those attributes will be copied into the subcollection documents.  This avoids `get()` calls in security rules, maintaining atomicity.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) is supported through structural segregation. Each top-level collection contains only documents with similar security requirements, ensuring that `list` operations can be secured effectively without complex filtering in rules.

Given that authentication will use `password` and `anonymous` methods, role-based access control will not be implemented using custom claims. If there is a need to limit access to any documents we will have to use DBAC.

The design uses descriptive naming conventions for all collections and documents, making it easy to understand the purpose of each part of the database. The structure leverages path-based ownership where appropriate (e.g., configuration).
